cards.title: Hello in Japanese
cards.body: In Japan, people greet each other with 'Konnichiwa', which roughly translates to 'good day' or 'hello'. The formal greeting is 'Ohayou gozaimasu' in the morning and 'Oyasumi nasai' at night.

cards.title: Counting in French
cards.body: In French, the numbers 70 to 79 are expressed using a combination of the numbers 60 and 10, due to the French Revolution's attempt to decimalize the language.

cards.title: Word Origin: 'Hello'
cards.body: The word 'hello' originated in the 19th century as a term used to attract attention, and was popularized by the introduction of the telephone.

cards.title: Linguistic Diversity
cards.body: There are over 7,000 languages spoken worldwide, with Indonesia having the most languages spoken within its borders, at over 700.

cards.title: Mandarin Chinese Tones
cards.body: Mandarin Chinese has four main tones, with the same word having different meanings based on the tone used, such as 'ma' meaning 'mother', 'hemp', 'horse', or 'scold'.

cards.title: Arabic Script
cards.body: The Arabic script is written from right to left, and is the second most widely used writing system in the world, with over 300 million people using it.

cards.title: Portuguese Pronunciation
cards.body: Portuguese has a unique pronunciation, with the 's' sound often being pronounced as a 'sh' sound, and the 'j' sound being pronounced as a soft 'zh' sound.

cards.title: Russian Alphabet
cards.body: The Russian alphabet, also known as Cyrillic, consists of 33 letters, with some letters similar to those in the Latin alphabet, but with distinct pronunciation.

cards.title: Korean Writing System
cards.body: The Korean writing system, known as Hangul, was created in the 15th century and consists of 14 consonants and 10 vowels, making it one of the most phonetic writing systems in the world.

cards.title: Word Origin: 'Goodbye'
cards.body: The word 'goodbye' originated from the phrase 'God be with you', which was used as a way to bid farewell, with the phrase being shortened over time.

cards.title: Spanish Dialects
cards.body: There are many dialects of Spanish, with the most common being Castilian Spanish, but other dialects such as Andalusian and Rioplatense also being widely spoken.

cards.title: German Grammar
cards.body: German grammar is known for its complexity, with four cases (nominative, accusative, genitive, and dative) and three main verb conjugations, making it a challenging language to learn.

quizzes.question: What is the formal greeting in Japan in the morning?
quizzes.explanation: Ohayou gozaimasu is the formal greeting in Japan in the morning.

quizzes.question: How many languages are spoken worldwide?
quizzes.explanation: There are over 7,000 languages spoken worldwide.

quizzes.question: What is unique about the Arabic script?
options: It is written from left to right
It is written from right to left
It is written from top to bottom
It is written from bottom to top
quizzes.explanation: The Arabic script is written from right to left.

quizzes.question: How many letters are in the Russian alphabet?
quizzes.explanation: The Russian alphabet, also known as Cyrillic, consists of 33 letters.
